entry.title: Re: How to remove balance carry forward from P&L - Plan/A...
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


The postings related to balance carryforward have period '000' and transaction type 'RFBC'. In order to remove these entries from reporting, you can filter to exclude period or transaction type in "P&L - Plan/Actuals" app. You can also refer to below KBA:

3590623 - How to remove Balance Carryforward from Controlling reports?


Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: HCM Brasil: Exclusivo Evento On-line da SAP sobre o Crédito do Trabalhador (eConsignado)
entry.content:
Boa tarde @dani_braga10 .

Ainda estamos avaliando se será possível contemplar o desconto parcial de forma automática na solução. A medida que formos evoluindo na solução, teremos mais certeza sobre como lidar com essas exceções.

 

Att. Leandro.

entry.title: Re: Reporte para Cobranza

entry.title: Re: Early Inspection Lot for Co-Product material.

entry.title: Re: Using standard jar files in CPI IFlow - Groovy script
entry.content:
Hi, Did you resolve this issue? I am also facing the same issue. Can you please let me know the resolution as early as possible? We are using SAP Cloud Connector and TCP Protocol to connect to AS400. Wrote a groovy script to make a connection with AS400. Getting this error "javax.script.ScriptException: java.lang.Exception: java.sql.SQLException: No suitable driver found for jdbc:as400://<VirtualHost>:<VirtualPort>;libraries=<DB_Name>@ line 47 in script1.groovy"

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Insert Link feature stopped working in SEN
entry.content: No, none of our links work anymore out of a Book Page to a media object in another workarea. Nothing happens when you click the link. This is new misbehavior in our Cloud SEN - it worked fine last week. All of our target URLs are published, all the way up to the Workspace top level, including the Resources. I also tried a link to a site on the open internet. Nothing happens in the published book page when you click the hyperlink. The book page and all its parent folders + Resources are also Published all the way up to the Workspace level. The HTML looks correct. Older book pages that I have not edited in a month or so still play correctly. But on the newer book page that I update weekly, none of the links work anymore. Presumably this is an issue in the new Cloud release 2503 (version 10.8.15 build 7020) that came out last week. I don't find any Knowledge Base Articles on it in me.sap.com though.

entry.title: Re: How to Archive Success Factors Goals and Performance Forms
entry.content: Thank you, that worked!

entry.title: Re: [SAP BTP Onboarding Series] - Step-by-Step guide to activate your SAP Build Work Zone, Advanced
entry.content:
Hi  Thank you for this excellent blog.I need help with a query please- 

Customer is using Microsoft entra as IdP. So we have this query, can we use Microsoft entra as a default IdP and use it as source system for user data in SAP build work zone advanced edition.

Or is it mandatory to have all the users to be set up in CIS to have access in SAP BWZ?

or in the step where in we set up IAS as source system in Identity provisioning, can we add entra as source system? in that case what about the user groups we create in CIS for Build work zone. 

@nageshcaparthy

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Cloud or On-Premise? Not an “either or” option, but a str
entry.content:
Hi @HolgerN,
for German standard scenarios, the cloud service on BTP (i.e. DRC cloud edition) does not retain any data - it only performs data transmission without storing any information. Even communication logs are taken back to SAP ERPs (any type) and stored there.
Hope it helps!
Best Regards,
Emanuele

Daniela

entry.title: Disable App Finder in S4HC
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

 

Our requirement is to disable app finder in S4HC Fiori page.

Thanks & Regards. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Disable App Finder in S4HC
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can disable the AppFinder by using the "Manage Launchpad Settings" application. With this app you can display and change parameters to configure different aspects of the launchpad. 

There are two parameters which are relevant for your case:

  • APPFINDER_ENABLED 
    Specify if your users have access to the app finder. If set to false, the app finder will only be available if personalization is enabled. Users can open all the apps in the app finder, which are assigned to them according to their role assignment. If set to true, the app finder is always available, even if the parameter HOMEPAGE_PERSONALIZATION is set to false.

  • HOMEPAGE_PERSONALIZATION
    Specify whether users can personalize the content displayed in pages.
    When set to false, the user can't perform the following actions:
    • Move, rename, delete, reset, or create sections
    • Move or remove tiles

    When false, the app finder is also disabled for users. But when the parameter APPFINDER_ENABLED is set to true, the app finder is always available, even if HOMEPAGE_PERSONALIZATION is set to false.

    Note that this setting doesn't influence My Home. When My Home is enabled (parameter SPACES_MYHOME), users can edit it anytime.

    See: Personalizing a Page

To disable the AppFinder, you need to disable the HOMEPAGE_PERSONALIZATION parameter. A prerequisite to access the Manage Launchpad Settings app is to have the SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR role assigned to your user. 

 

Please note that this will be applied for every user in your system. It's not possible to change this for particular users.

Also note that the AppFinder doesn't show additional tiles, it only shows the tiles that are assigned to the users. Therefore, it might be that you don't need to disable it at all 🙂 

 

For more information, please check SAP Help Portal | Manage Launchpad Settings and SAP Help Portal | App Finder

 

Best regards.

entry.title: DATA([d/Measures] = "Amount", [d/gl_account] = "RevToVolumsap analytics cloud advanced formula issue
entry.content:
Hi 

Sorry , by mistake I deleted script, reposting the same. Here Third data point not getting calculated. 

DATA([d/Measures] = "Amount", [d/gl_account] = "RevToVolume", [d/data_src] = "calc") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/data_src] = "Input", [d/gl_account] = "RevToVolume", [d/product_grp] = "#", [d/product] = "#", [d/profit_center] = "#", [d/posting_month] = "000000")
DATA([d/Measures] = "Amount", [d/gl_account] = "TPV", [d/data_src] = "calc") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/gl_account]="TPV",[d/data_src]= "fba")
DATA([d/Measures] = "Amount", [d/gl_account] = "TTR_STAT", [d/data_src] = "calc") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Amount", [d/gl_account]="TPV",[d/data_src]= "calc") * RESULTLOOKUP( [d/Measures]="Amount", [d/gl_account] = "RevToVolume", [d/data_src] = "calc")

 

Regards

Mahesh

entry.title: Re: What Is SAP Business Data Cloud? Benefits, Architecture & Use Cases
entry.content:
@MustafaBensan 

  1. Is all replicated data, both from SAP and non-SAP sources, stored as files in the Foundation Service Data Lake? --> Foundation layer can be object storage or in HANA Cloud .
  2. To make this data available in Datasphere for analysis, does it need to be replicated again into Datasphere, or will Datasphere directly access the Foundation Service Data Lake files?--> Datasphere will not have its own storage it will be dependent on foundation layer. So, we don't need to replicate from foundation layer as far as we use datasphere from BDC.

Thanks

Dinesh

entry.content:

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Cloud or On-Premise? Not an “either or” option, but a str
entry.content: @peterczo thank you for your comment. This is to take advantage of latest technologies as they continue to progress, to make setup effort and ongoing maintenance simpler and simpler for our customers (SAP maintains as a service instead of having to deploy integration flows in the customer environment). Across all products, we continue to invest to improve what we can offer.

entry.title: Re: Get Data from https://smard.api.bund.dev/ not working (complete ABAP-Code is in post)
entry.content:
The data you get from get_cdata is binary data. You have to translate it into your program internal data.

First you can translate it into readable data to see that you have the right data, for instance using method CL_BINARY_CONVERT=>XSTRING_UTF8_TO_STRING.

You can map the binary data using "CALL TRANSFORMATION" to program internal data. Maybe a "Simple Transformation" does the job for you (https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_751_index_htm/7.51/en-US/abenabap_st.htm).

Or use the sXML library to analyze the data (https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_751_index_htm/7.51/en-US/abenabap_sxml_lib.htm).

There may be other ways to analyze the json data.

entry.title: Re: What configuration need to CHECK PRINT in SAP S4HANA Public cloud ?
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, configuring Check Printing involves several key steps to ensure smooth processing. Here’s a high-level overview of the necessary configuration: 1. Maintain Payment Medium Format • Go to Manage Your Solution (SSCUI). • Search for "Define Payment Medium Formats". • Ensure that the format "SAPCheck" or a country-specific format is maintained for check printing. 2. Configure Payment Method for Checks • Navigate to "Manage Your Solution" > Configure Payment Methods by Country/Region. • Select your country and ensure the payment method for Checks (e.g., C for Check) is defined with the correct payment medium format. 3. Assign Check Number Ranges • Go to "Manage Bank Accounts" in Fiori. • Select the bank account used for check printing. • Maintain the Check Number Range under the payment method settings. 4. Assign Check Printer and Layout • Access "Output Parameter Determination" (App ID: APP ID: F2121). • Define Output Type for check printing (e.g., CHECK). • Maintain the Form Template (Adobe Forms-based) for printing. • Assign the Printer for check output. 5. Test Check Printing • Execute Automatic Payment Run (App ID: F110). • Ensure that the generated checks are printed correctly via "Manage Payments".

entry.title: SAP ALM Roadshow Palo Alto, California Wrap up March 4th 2025!
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce the remarkable success of the SAP ALM Roadshow 2025, held recently in Palo Alto, CA, North America. These events provided an exceptional opportunity for enterprises to explore the latest advancements in Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) alongside SAP experts, industry leaders, and representatives from various SAP Lines of Business (LoBs).

Event Highlights:

  • Opening Address: The event was inaugurated with a keynote speech by Mr. Naren Baliga, Head of Premium Engagements – Delivery, West.
  • Industry Insights: Mrs. Smitha Banda, Senior Consulting Manager and BTP Practice Lead NA, shared valuable insights and industry trends focusing on "Clean Core and RISE with SAP Methodology."

Topics Covered:

  • SAP Cloud ALM for GROW with SAP
  • SAP Cloud ALM for RISE with SAP and Clean Core Support
  • Implementing Intelligent Enterprise with SAP Cloud ALM
  • Automating Testing with Tricentis Test Automation
  • Optimizing Operations by Combining AI and SAP Cloud ALM
  • Leveraging SAP Focused Run for Advanced Monitoring Capabilities
  • Transitioning from Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM

Key Highlights:

  1. Innovative Workshops: Participants engaged in dynamic and interactive workshops showcasing the latest SAP ALM tools and methodologies tailored for the North American market.
  2. Expert Insights: Mr. Umesh Jagadesh, Regional Lead RGTM NA LAC, provided insights on how SAP Cloud ALM can be leveraged during customers' RISE and GROW journeys and industry trends in utilizing SAP Cloud ALM.
  3. Regional Case Studies: Local success stories were presented, demonstrating how companies effectively implemented SAP ALM solutions to drive business growth and efficiency.
  4. Tech Demos: Live demonstrations of advanced SAP Cloud ALM features showcased solutions designed to propel organizations forward.
  5. Networking Sessions: Attendees seized valuable networking opportunities to connect with peers, exchange experiences, and build professional relationships.
  6. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Representatives from various SAP LoBs, including Cloud ERP, RISE with SAP, and BTP, contributed to the discussions, offering a comprehensive view of SAP’s ecosystem and its integration with SAP Cloud ALM.

Event Participation:

The event in Palo Alto, CA, held on March 4th, 2025, welcomed over 22 customers, fostering enriching discussions and collaborations.

Looking Ahead:

The positive feedback from the Palo Alto SAP ALM Roadshow Event underscores the significance of regional engagements in meeting specific market needs. In response to your valuable input, we are actively working on the feedback received and will soon return with deep dive sessions in the West, designed to further explore critical topics and enhance the learning experience.

Conclusion:

The SAP ALM Roadshow Palo Alto 2025 has set a new benchmark for innovation and collaboration in the North American region. We extend our gratitude to all participants, as well as our collaborative partners from Cloud ERP, RISE with SAP, and BTP, for contributing to this success. A heartfelt thanks to Travis Harvey, Head of Customer Success and Delivery - West, for his outstanding leadership and support, and to Jordan Godin, Technical Quality Manager - West, for his unwavering commitment to making this event a resounding success. We eagerly look forward to building on this achievement together.

entry.title: C_TS4FI_2023 practice questions
entry.content: I'm looking for C_TS4FI_2023 practice questions. Could anyone share reliable study materials or insights from a recent successful attempt?

entry.title: Fast Restore for SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake Relational Engine
entry.content:
One of the challenges in managing a really large database is the amount of time it can take to restore the database. This is particularly true if you have to physically copy the backup files from the backup storage to the production server. Prior to the 2024-QRC4 release of SAP HANA Cloud, data lake relational engine ("HDLRE") we were able to execute a database recovery at a rate of approximately 1 TB/hour. At that speed, recovering an 80 TB HDLRE database was taking more than 3 full days and this was a pretty clear problem for any SAP IQ customers looking to migrate their on premise systems to HDLRE.

With the 2024-QRC4 release of HDLRE, changes were made to how backup versions of database pages are managed and database restore times are now independent of the database size. 

What changed?

When updates are made to a table in an HDLRE database, the database pages that have been modified are written out as new objects to the underlying native cloud object storage. Prior to the 2024-QRC4 release, the old versions of the database pages would be physically deleted as soon as the new page versions were written. Backup snapshots of these old database pages would be kept by the object store for the configured retention period (default 14 days, configurable up to 7 months) and could be physically restored if needed. This was a nice clean approach because it cleaned up the old page versions immediately, rather than relying on a 'garbage collection' process to delete them later on. The downside was that restoring the database required physically checking every database page to see if it had a snapshot that needed to be restored, and then executing the restore for any deleted page versions. This implementation meant that the recovery time was determined by the size of the database. 

With the 2024-QRC4 release, deletion of the old database page versions from the object store is deferred based on the backup retention period. For example, if the database backup retention period is configured to be the default 14 days, then once a given database page is modified, the old page version will be kept for 14 days before being physically deleted. Now, when a database is restored the only piece of the database that needs to be physically restored is the IQ_SYSTEM_MAIN which is where the database page list is maintained. The individual database table pages don't have to be physically restored because they haven't been deleted yet. 

The size of IQ_SYSTEM_MAIN is effectively independent of the the data volume in the database tables. Restoring the IQ_SYSTEM_MAIN for a 100 TB (or larger) database doesn't take any longer than it does for a 1 TB database. Since the only thing that has to be physically copied now to restore and HDLRE database is the IQ_SYSTEM_MAIN, the time required to restore is now independent of the database size. The time needed to restore an 80 TB database is now measured in minutes, not days.

entry.title: Re: C_TS4FI_2023 practice questions
entry.content: For C_TS4FI_2023 practice questions, Certs4Future is a great resource. They provide updated and verified study materials to help you prepare effectively. Their practice tests cover key exam topics and mirror real exam scenarios, making it easier to boost confidence and pass on your first attempt. Check out Certs4Future for the right study materials!

entry.title: Internal server error exam appointments
entry.content:
Hi there 

I am not able to access EXAM APPOINTMENTS in sapcertification.questionmark.com. It gives me an Internal Server Error (500) - see screenshot attached.

Due to this I cant reschedule / cancel already scheduled exams!

It has been happening since 2 days already; I have raised a ticket 5000139316 in SAP Learning Self Service(s) https://lso-ssrv-app.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/lso/

Has anyone else faced this issue?

Thanks

Arijit Das

entry.title: Re: C_TS4FI_2023 practice questions
entry.content: If you're preparing for the C_TS4FI_2023 exam, Passexamhub offers reliable and up-to-date practice questions to help you succeed. Their study materials align with SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting concepts, ensuring you grasp key topics like financial closing, asset accounting, and reporting. Many candidates have found their resources effective in passing the exam on the first attempt. Check out Passexamhub for structured study materials and boost your confidence for the exam!

entry.title: Re: using selection screen tabbed block how to bind SALV,...
entry.content:
You need to define your subscreen using the screen painter (no selection screen subscreen).

For the definition of the subscreen and creation of alv at this screen look at example program SALV_DEMO_TABLE_SIMPLE with screen 100 (attention: this is not defined as subscreen, but you need a subscreen).

entry.title: Re: using selection screen tabbed block how to bind SALV,...
entry.content:
You would not be able to achieve this using normal selection screen "sub screen". You would need to create a module pool programming with main and sub screens created through screen painter of the program. On the newly created sub screen, you need to insert custom control. Then you can fill the custom control using ALV grid through container. You can make those sub screens as your tab strips. Check out the below link which has more specific details. Demo Program BCALV_GRID_DEMO.  

Creating an ALV Grid Control

entry.title: Re: Using Fiori apps Project Cost Overview and Project Cost Line Items for Project Reporting
entry.content:
Dear @andrea_langlotz,

this topic is very valuable. I would like to share with you a current need. My client would like to obtain, in the project reports, information regarding the Supllier code,  invoice number (MIRO) and the document reference number. I thought it would be possible to retrieve this information in the Project Cost Line Items app (F6992) in SAP Cloud. However, this information is not available. I accessed other apps related to WBS elements but was also unsuccessful. My question is more about confirming whether we actually don't have any app that provides this information in a standard way? Should we proceed with a customized solution, for example, via queries?

 

Looking forward to your response.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Related Request for Change (ChaRm UI field) not appea...
entry.content: In the result set view, first check what is the field name and data source. Sometimes, there might be value attributes which are not stored in database but calculated during runtime. If it's value attribute, then you cannot directly assign to search query. But, if it is an model attribute and has entity relationship, then you can add it to search query. I don't have access to Charm system to check this out. So, maybe if you can share the field name F2 screenshot from result set column, I can help. If you are not able to find this in browser, go to BSP_WD_CMPWB transaction in the GUI system and open the component/view to check those fields from configuration.

entry.title: CJ20N: Zugriffskontrollliste (ACL)
entry.content:
Hallo, 

ich möchte gerne erreichen, dass nur bestimmte Personen auf die Projektdaten Zugriff haben.
Laut meiner momentaner Recherche ist das durch die Aktivierung von ACL im Projektprofil möglich. 
Wie kann ich dann erreichen, dass nur eine bestimmte User Gruppe, die Berechtigung besitzt die Projektdaten einzusehen ? 
Als ich Testweise ein Projekt mit ACL angelegt hatte, war ich der einzige der diese Daten einsehen konnte. 
Ich wäre für jede Erläuterung zum Vorgehen sehr dankbar.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to create auto PO and STO both in SAP...
entry.content:
When you create ZNB -- did you remove all related PO - Linkage of Requisition Doc. Type to Purchasing Document Type and maintained ZNB mapped to ZNB PO Type ? also ZUB mapped to ZUB STO type ?

Also, check what your configuration is for For stock transport orders, the document type is derived from the supplying plant/receiving plant combination (table T161W or Customizing transaction OMGN).

114213 - ME59: Determining the order document type - SAP for Me

3108114 - Default document type is not used when creating PO via ME59N - SAP for Me-- this KBA suggest use ME_BSART_DET

entry.title: Re: While creating PR using tcode ME51N an error showing...
entry.content:
Are your users on VMWare GUI or do they have Local SAP GUI ?--Also are using any specific material when this error is happening ?--you want to review the long text and remove any special characters from the long text--also look at --it may resolve by implementing corrections instructions from one of these.

1612594 - CA01/02 : Long text - Error TD605 with special characters - SAP for Me

1991022 - GP12N - error TD605 appears during longtext creation - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: DATA([d/Measures] = "Amount", [d/gl_account] = "RevTo...
entry.content:
@MK70 

Multiplication and Division in SAC Advance formula works as a join between all dimension of the 2 resultlookups. What this means is you need to specify all non common dimensions between the 2 resultlookups explicitly.

Easiest way would be 2 drilldown to the lowest level of the 2 RESULTLOOKUPs being multiplied and then mention all non common dimensions explicitly

For e.g if one data set is on date 00000 and others on valid date, you have to ensure you have date specified explicitly in 1 lookup

RESULTLOOKUP([d/Date]="0000")*RESULTLOOKUP()

Just compare the 2 lookups and look for all dimensions and see what are not commons (dimensions with different master data) between them.

I will try to explain with an example

Suppose I want to multiply the below2 set of records

dataset 1

CCPCAccountProductMeasure
CC1PC1RateP110
CC2PC2RateP215
CC3PC3RateP320
CC4PC4RateP425

Dataset 2

CCPCAccountProductMeasure
##Disc%#0.1

Result

CCPCAccountProductMeasure
CC1PC1DiscP11
CC2PC2DiscP21.5
CC3PC3DiscP32
CC4PC4DiscP42.5

DATA([d/Account]="Disc")=RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account]="Rate") * RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account]="Disc%", [d/CC]="#",[d/PC]="#",[d/Product]="#")

Focus How I had to specify CC,PC and Product in my second RESULTLOOKUP as they are non common dimension between the 2 dataset.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Error no accounts maintained for company code clearin...
entry.content:
You may not complete the required setup 

2425601 - Error F5730 in VL02N when posting Goods Issue - SAP ERP & SAP S/4 HANA - SAP for Me

Maintain the G/L Account for Inter-Company transfers between Company Code A and Company Code B  in transaction OBYA

entry.title: Re: Mark vendor for deletion

ich bin seit einiger Zeit auf der Suche nach einer detaillierten Dokumentation oder Erklärung, die das Zusammenspiel zwischen Systemstatus und Benutzerstatus näher erläutert und die verschiedenen Einstellungsmöglichkeiten beschreibt (z. B. Objekttypen, Geschäftstransaktionen, automatische Änderung des Benutzerstatus bei …).

entry.title: Re: Using standard jar files in CPI IFlow - Groovy script
entry.content: Hi, I wrote a Groovy script in CPI Iflow to connect directly with AS400 server. Uploaded JT400.jar and db2jcc4.jar in the Iflow / References tab. But groovy is throwing the error: javax.script.ScriptException: java.lang.Exception: java.sql.SQLException: No suitable driver found for jdbc:as400://<Virtual_Host>:<Virtual_Port>;libraries=<Library_Name>@ line 47 in script1.groovy. We are using SAP Cloud Connector and TCP Protocol to connect to AS400. Can you please help in letting me know the resolution? Class.forName('com.ibm.as400.access.AS400JDBCDriver') Driver driver = new com.ibm.as400.access.AS400JDBCDriver(); DriverManager.registerDriver(driver); url = 'jdbc:as400://<Virtual_Host>:<Virtual_Port>;libraries=<Library_Name>'; connection = DriverManager.getConnection(url, user, password) Thanks in advance, Mageswari M.

entry.title: Employee Data api from H4S4 to Non SAP Solution
entry.content:
Hi

We are not in SF-EC but use H4S4. We need to send Employee Data via api in real time from H4S4 to a Non SAP Solution.

Are there any std api available or we need a S4 Event addon?

Regards

entry.title: Project planned cost
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We are planning a new project where we have materials planned from stock and without from stock check box enabled. For the products where we have from stock enabled we could see the inventory cost has taken for the planned cost, but for the products where we have not enable from stock the planned cost is determined from the purchase related documents.
Is there a way for us to determine the valuation method for the planned cost of the material in the projects? Like we need inventory cost as the valuation method for all scenarios.

Thanks,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Project planned cost
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

The planned cost determination for materials in projects can be influenced by several factors, including whether materials are planned with or without the "From Stock" checkbox enabled. When materials are planned "from stock," the system uses the inventory cost for valuation, as it reflects the actual cost of the stock already present.

Verify that the material master data is set up correctly, with proper valuation methods defined. This could involve setting specific valuation classes or defining pricing conditions that prioritize inventory costing

You can find more details in the Help Documents Below and KBA for further information:

Sourcing Material from Stock for Projects
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2cdc27bb722d10148129eef977ccbb37.html?locale=en-US

Project Cost and Revenue Planning
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2cc80d4d722d1014af65a4e0c365c1a8.html?locale=en-US

KBA: 2831120 - The Project Planned Cost Shows Zero After Maintaining Material or service in the Project https://me.sap.com/notes/2831120

entry.title: Re: Gateway Timeout CAP application - is there a paramete...
entry.content:
We need to add timeout on the approuter

https://nocin.eu/cap-timeout-on-long-running-endpoint/

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/get-started/troubleshooting#why-are-long-running-requests-rejected-with-status-504-after-30-seconds-even-though-the-application-continues-processing-the-request

entry.title: Re: Generating SAP Fiori table maintenance dialogs in SAP S/4HANA 2021
entry.content:
Hi Andre, thank you for sharing this very useful information. I think someone already asked this question - Is it possible to use this feature for tables where deliver class = A (master or transaction data)? We have a situation where the user needs to update data in some of the columns manually. If not, is there any other faster way to achieve this.

Appreciate your inputs/suggestion.

Thanks

Sunny

entry.title: SAP HANA virtualization with proxmox
entry.content:
I would like to inquire about the experience of those who work with SAP Hana and virtual proxmox. Specifically, I am interested in hearing about any challenges you have encountered, either with the SAP client or in general.
I would like to ensure that I will not encounter any issues while using proxmox in the future.

entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950
entry.content:
Hi @Dhood,

Have you activated this since you are in ECC ? In Control Cycle -EWM Staging ?

  • On the SAP Easy Access screen for Extended Warehouse Management, choose Master Data  Production Supply Area  Define PSA.

  • You have activated the business function EWM Integration in Manufacturing 2 (LOG_PP_EWM_MAN_2) in SAP ERP.

    For more information, in SAP ERP, see transaction SFW5.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EXTENDED_WAREHOUSE_MANAGEMENT/3d97bec9bf1649099384bb8167df3cf2/a39aeb535cbb5d1ee10000000a441470.html

Please create an OSS Note If issue still persists.

Kind regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: An in-depth look at clean core business processes, read our Clean Core for S/4 HANA Cloud paper!
entry.content: Amazing post! Already reading the white paper, awesome content, thanks a lot for sharing and propagate that!

entry.title: Re: How to attach the uploaded files of sap BPA to email
entry.content: Hi Aye, Are you able to access the uploaded file in your process flow, and take actions on it?

entry.title: No data fields in email template to add direct link to approve / reject PR item from email
entry.content:
I have configured flexible workflow for item level release of purchase requisition items (WS02000471) and set up the corresponding scenario email template (YY1_02000471_CRT_ALL). It is a copy from the pre-delivered template SWF_CRT_NOTIFY_RECIPIENTS.

How to add approve/reject buttons/link to offline approval?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: No data fields in email template to add direct link t...
entry.content:
Hello,

Following KBA 3491173, it is possible to configure a direct link to the My Inbox URL for that work item using CDS view I_WorkflowTaskURL and field WorkflowTaskURL.

 

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Insert Link feature stopped working in SEN
entry.content:
Hi Mani,

I've just had a look and there is definitely an error being generated when you click a link, including for external links.  Can you please raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-MGR and get support involved.

Thanks
Shane

entry.title: Re: SUSG integration with ATC
entry.content:
Hi @KumarAakash,

Based on your questions, your interests seem specific to the custom code adjustments required to convert to SAP S/4HANA. Therefore, I recommend the Custom Code Migration Guide for SAP S/4HANA 2023 guide.

Best Regards,
Greg

entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950
entry.content: Did you release the process order after creation ?

entry.title: Re: User authorization is not working in BEX Query

Hi 

Is the company code characteristic the relevant authorization? if it yes,then you can specific the company code.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: With the new Full People Profile have a section like...
entry.content: Userid is not available in the profile, and regarding username, login name instead of username is displaying next to employee name. It requires permission: User Account OData entity. Could you please grant the permission and then check if it is visible?

entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950
entry.content: Ajit - When I create the Process Order via COR1 tcode, the order is already in a REL CRCR status. I can't find another tcode to use for the release. As for the PSA in EWM, I have already maintained this as well as the business functions. I have followed the Advanced Production Integration config guide from SAP.

entry.title: Re: sap gui installation issue
entry.content:
Hi

Support for SAPGUI 7.60 ended in 2022. You can try the latest version, SAPGUI 800, along with the most recent patch.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: additional Application Server
entry.content:
Hi Sim,

Have you initiated the additional application instance using the SAP SIDADM user ID? Please ensure you are not using the root user ID.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: SUSG integration with ATC
entry.content:
Hi @KumarAakash ,

To echo on @manuel_horn3 's reply, in the blog you can find [all custom code analysis options at one glance]:
1. Remote ATC with SAP GUI(required system SAP_BASIS 752)
2. Remote ATC with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration(required system SAP S/4HANA >=1809)
3.Remote ATC with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration(required system SAP BTP ABAP Environment). 

So if you don't have S/4HANA system or SAP BTP, but have ECC system on SAP_BASIS 752 or 753, you can consider using Option 1. Remote ATC with SAP GUI. This is also mentioned in Readiness Checking note 2913617:
"The ABAP test cockpit is an alternative custom code analysis tool supported by SAP Readiness Check. The ABAP test cockpit will perform a more in-depth analysis in comparison to the Custom Code Analyzer. However, it must be highlighted that there are additional steps required to enable this analysis. The process for establishing an ABAP test cockpit system for performing this analysis is documented here.

SAP Note 2781766 must be implemented on the system where the ABAP test cockpit is installed and enabled. If that system is >= SAP_BASIS 7.53 SP3 or >= SAP_BASIS 7.52 SP5, SAP Note 2781766 is not required, as the functionality is already available in those releases.

To create the ZIP archive in the ABAP test cockpit for upload to SAP Readiness Check, select the context menu option Export to -> File for SAP Readiness Check in the ABAP test cockpit result browser.

Similar to the Customer Code Analyzer, the ABAP test cockpit analysis is conducted outside of the program RC_COLLECT_ANALYSIS_DATA and is later uploaded to the analysis created on the landing page of SAP Readiness Check."

Best Regards,
David

entry.title: Hide Transport Change document type from Solution documentation dropdown
entry.content:
We are using Change control for Solution documentation, and we are using a separate transaction type(ZMAD – DIGITS*) to make the changes in SolDoc apart from the change document that will be used for transports(ZMMJ- DIGITS*).

We would like to hide the change document types that are used for transports (ZMMJ) from the change document dropdown in SolDoc but would like to refer documentation in them (manual assignment through single selection and multiple selection buttons). Please suggest if this is possible.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Hide Transport Change document type from Solution doc...
entry.content: The function you require is not included in the SAP standard system for Solution Manager 7.2.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Public Cloud Group reporting why does mast...
entry.content: You only need to specify the global parameter when a new user logs in for the first time or if a change is required. In all other cases, it doesn’t need to be re-entered.

entry.title: Re: How can we change Labels for a separate Site, without...
entry.content:
Hello @Nehar ,

Unfortunately, Currently it is not possible to update labels for a particular site.

Updating the label would affect home page for both users. Internal and External.

You may raise an enhancement request around this if you think this feature might be helpful and should be a part of SuccessFactors Learning.

 If this information helped you then please consider giving Kudos or accepting this post as solution.

Swapnil

entry.title: Re: Sample File Link on Detail Screen - Agent Performance Management
entry.content: Not specific to a broker/customer. I want the sample file be added to the form so that it's visible in every record. I did not see any option to add a link in customised forms so was looking for alternatives.

entry.title: Screen persona profiler or similar tool for Zebra RF devices
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We build and use sap screen persona flavors on Zebra RF devices, flavors are built for transaction /SCWM/RFUI. For many screen users are complaining that screen loads very slowly and took more time. Screen for which user is complaining does have any script, I tried to use same flavors on laptop, and it do some slowness but when see same flavors on RF device it really takes more time. So is there any tool or way available which could help locating the object which is causing this delay?

I am searching for a tool which could help me analyzing the performance of flavors when we run it on Zebra mobile devices. I tried activating "Screen persona profiler", its visible on laptop browser but I do not see this option when flavor is launched on mobile screen. Is there a way to activate it for mobile devices? 

If not is there any other tool or available in market which could integrate with SAP without putting any extra load and help analyzing the performance?

Apricate your replies, thoughts and ideas. 

Thanks in advance. 

Regards

Ritz

entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950
entry.content: That is correct. PMR should create without staging component.

entry.title: Re: Sales Return Copy Data
entry.content: From April 1, divisions 20 and 21 will be closed. Since sales were invoiced under these divisions, SAP copies all header data when creating a return order through billing document reference. new requirement is, from April 1 onward, any return order for these divisions should be processed under the new division 22.

else do we have any SAP accounts to test the sample einvoicing? 

Thank you

entry.title: Cost planning in Cj40 is not availble in Cj30 to distribute
entry.content: User has plan the cost in Cj40 for WBS element ST-0138-L , however in Cj30 when trying to distribute the cost that WBS element is greyed out and cost is not available to distribute . Please help ti understand why this is happening.

entry.title: 8 Baby Steps to Learn Joule - (Step #2) How Joule Works
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series 8 Baby Steps to Learn Joule. In the previous blog #1 – Introduction to Joule, we learnt what Joule is and how customers can use it. 

In this post, we will learn how Joule works.

Let's begin!

 

 

Step 1 – User Enters the Prompt

Users start with their queries in the natively integrated Joule client embedded in SAP cloud solutions, such as S/4HANA, SuccessFactors etc.

 

Step 2 – The request is processed and orchestrated by Joule

1. First, Joule analyses if the prompt is relevant for Joule’s Scenario Catalog.

What is Joule's Scenario Catalog?

Joule's Scenario Catalog is a comprehensive repository that contains metadata of all available scenarios, functions, and skills across SAP cloud applications. When a user interacts with Joule, their input is first analysed against this catalog to determine if it aligns with any predefined scenarios or functionalities. This process ensures that Joule can provide accurate and contextually relevant responses based on the capabilities defined within the SAP ecosystem.

2. Further, Joule conducts an informational filtering based on the Knowledge Catalog. This process is based on RAG and lets the LLM generate an answer which is contextually relevant and precise. 

Knowledge Catalog is a repository that contains SAP’s proprietary knowledge and customer owned knowledge via Document Grounding.

3. Joule is aware of the user's context and chat history.

This includes information about which SAP cloud application the respective user is using and which additional SAP application the customer has licensed and activated with Joule.

Also, Joule is aware of the user's role(s) and permissions. This means a user cannot access information or adjust business processes they couldn't do directly in the SAP cloud application without using Joule.

 

Step 3 – Joule sends an enhanced query to LLM

Joule takes all of this information and provides a much richer query to the LLM.

These LLMs are also available directly via SAP’s Generative AI Hub, which is part of the SAP BTP.

 

Which LLM is used by Joule?

Joule follows SAP‘s general multi-partner strategy for generative AI.  Joule may employ a variety of foundational models to deliver the best performance for each use case. This ensures that customers get benefitted from the most relevant LLM.

Joule leverages only those LLMs which are available for SAP customer and partners within Generative AI Hub in AI Foundation in SAP BTP.

SAP has under strict contractual agreements with LLM providers which prohibits them from training any of their AI-models on any SAP’s customer data.

 

Step 4 – LLM’s response back to Joule

Next, the LLM provides a grounded response back to the Joule service.

This is:

  • Either the response based on its training data and knowledge which could be sent directly to the user
  • or there is a need to leverage Joule functions which includes the Joule scenarios of SAP Cloud Applications.

In simple words, if the user’s query requires action within an SAP system or information from SAP system, the LLM identifies the need to call Joule functions, which connect to SAP Cloud Applications (e.g., S/4HANA, SuccessFactors).

 

Step 5 – Joule Calls the SAP Backend System

If required, Joule leverages Joule functions to call an SAP backend system.

1. Joule sends the request to the relevant SAP backend system.

For this, the respective information is retrieved via Odata calls OR the LLM for dialog management calls another LLM which was used by an SAP Cloud Application to develop a specific scenario (e.g. interview question generation).

2. Joule gets the required information, processes it.

3. The response from SAP is also filtered and validated to ensure it meets enterprise security, data privacy, and responsible AI guidelines. This step ensures that only secure, accurate, and appropriate responses are sent back to the user.

 

Step 6 - Final Response to User

The user receives a response, knowing that the conversation and output has been processed all with enterprise security, data privacy, and responsible AI considered.

 

This is in nutshell - How Joule works!

 

𝐃𝐫𝐨𝐩 𝐚 "𝐘𝐞𝐬" 𝐢𝐧 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬 𝐢𝐟 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐮𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐜𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐥𝐲 👇

 

Stay tuned for Blog #3 - Joule Architecture.  Meanwhile, you may read the blog Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

entry.title: Re: Fixed Asset:Posting Acquisitions without Order
entry.content: Dear Customer, Please Activate Direct Posting to G/L & Material Accounts & Assets (ID in the ‘Configure Your Solution’ configuration environment: 106330). Please refer to the KBA 3369087 - Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced (MIRO) app - missing Asset tab (https://me.sap.com/notes/3369087) for more details. Best Regards, Evelyn

entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950
entry.content:
Hi @Dhood ,

PMR is designed as per below process. and for GR, you can create EGR process if you want.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EXTENDED_WAREHOUSE_MANAGEMENT/3d97bec9bf1649099384bb8167df3cf2/1b7fec53d3eb5514e10000000a441470.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EXTENDED_WAREHOUSE_MANAGEMENT/3d97bec9bf1649099384bb8167df3cf2/c399eb535cbb5d1ee10000000a441470.html

Production Material Request

Definition

The production material request is a document containing all the warehouse-relevant data for a manufacturing order (a production order or process order).

Use

You use the production material request for planning and monitoring the supply of products to production, and for the consumption of products by production, using advanced production integration.

The system creates a production material request when SAP ERP sends a message containing the required information from production. SAP ERP sends this message when the manufacturing order is released, and WM material staging is triggered, either automatically or in the Production Order Change transaction, either by choosing Function  WM Material Staging  Execute, or by clicking the WM Material Staging button.

If the manufacturing order is changed in SAP ERP, the system updates the production material request in SAP EWM for changes that are relevant for SAP EWM, such as a changed quantity for staging.

To view production material requests, on the SAP Easy Access screen for Extended Warehouse Management, choose Work Scheduling  Staging for Production  Maintain Production Material Request.

 
Please let us know if further questions.
 
Kind Regards,
Ajit

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud compatibility with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Is there any feature support matrix for SAP Analytics Cloud functionality for Datasphere (direct consume for analytics model)?

We have done few testing and found that nice feature such Compass, Predictive Scenario, In chart time series forecast and smart insight is not working.

Thanks for all help.

entry.title: Re: How to attach the uploaded files of sap BPA to email
entry.content: You can't upload files directly to the email notification in the process flow. for such kind of thing you will need to create separate automation.

entry.title: Re: SAC Story date Issue
entry.content: would you please rephrase your question a bit? It's difficult to understand.

entry.title: Fiori-Style Analytics Dashboard: 'Invalid Username/Password' Error for Non-Manager Users
entry.content:
After logging into SAP Business One, our system automatically loads the Fiori-style Analytics dashboard (Pervasive Analytics). While the Manager user has no issues accessing the dashboard, other users consistently receive an "Invalid Username/Password" error when the dashboard loads.

Details:

  • SAP Business One Version: Sap Business One 10 FP 2502
  • Database: SAP HANA (SLD connection user is SYSTEM)  - version Hana 2.00.059
  • Issue: After successful login to SAP Business One, the Fiori-style dashboard attempts to load and fails for non-manager users with the error "Invalid Username/Password".
  • Manager User: Works perfectly without any errors.
  • Other Users: Despite having the required permissions for analytics, they are facing authentication issues only with the dashboard.

What I've Checked/Done:

  • User Permissions: Verified that all affected users have the necessary authorizations for Analytics and Pervasive Analytics.
  • SLD & Database Connection: Confirmed that the SLD database connection user is SYSTEM, which has all required privileges.

My Questions:

  1. What could cause the "Invalid Username/Password" error for non-manager users when accessing the Fiori-style dashboard, given that the Manager user works fine??
  2. Are there any known configuration settings or workarounds for ensuring that all users benefit from the SYSTEM user's privileges (like PAL_ROLE and AFL execution rights) when accessing Analytics?

entry.title: Re: DMR in Inspection lot vs Inspection Plan
entry.content: @Vignesh V May I ask where you captured the 1st screenshot on the DMR on characteristic level, please?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud compatibility with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi ,

Yes, there is a feature support matrix that outlines the functionalities available when connecting SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) directly to SAP Datasphere for analytics models. Establishing a live connection between SAC and SAP Datasphere allows you to leverage real-time data without replication. However, it's important to be aware of certain limitations and restrictions associated with this setup.

Analytics Limitations:

  • Map Visualizations: Only bubble, heat, choropleth, and flow maps are supported.
  • Data Blending: Merging data is restricted to datasets within the same SAP Datasphere space. Cross-space or cross-tenant data blending is not supported. Additionally, blending SAP Datasphere data with other live connections, such as SAP BW, is not possible.​
  • Advanced Features: Features like scheduling publications, R visualizations, and comment widgets are not supported. Linked dimensions are limited to models within the same space; cross-space linking is unavailable.

Also refer this note : SAP Note 2832606 – Unsupported Features with SAP Datasphere

Hope this helps. Thank you

regards

Giri

entry.title: Re: Work Center Auto Update in PM Order Confirmation
entry.content: Hi @Vaibav-Mishra, apologies for the delayed response, and thank you for your thoughtful input on my post—it’s much appreciated! That’s exactly what I’m aiming to achieve. The employee will carry out the work, and their ID will be populated accordingly. The confirmation will align with the work center linked to this employee, which could differ from the originally planned one. Additionally, no individual is assigned to more than one work center.

entry.title: Re: DVM Cloud App values vs DBACOCKPIT (HANA DB Memory Us...
entry.content:
Hi @jan_kotrc 

The used HANA Memory Usage(GB) = DRAM + Persistent Memory

For more information regarding Persistent Memory, please refer to the following note:
2700084 - FAQ: SAP HANA Persistent Memory

Best Regards,
David

entry.title: How to resend failed messages in S/4HANA Public Cloud to SAP FSM
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm implementing the integration package - Integration of Service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Field Service Management.

I have some failed messages of the integration flow - Replicate Service Order from SAP S4HANA Cloud to SAP Field Service Management because of the data error in SAP FSM. Now the data in FSM has been corrected.

How can I resend these failed messages from S4 to FSM?

Regards,

Minh

entry.title: Re: Query: Scope item BD6- Basic Credit Management

entry.title: Re: AS ABAP 752 SP04, developer edition: NOW AVAILABLE
entry.content:
Hi, 

Do anyone successfully installed the new license?? 

I tried but is not working for me so It would be helpful to confirm that new license works for You, 

Kind Regards

Roberto

entry.title: Re: How to resend failed messages in S/4HANA Public Cloud...
entry.content:
Dear Minh,

Could you please check the Last Section mentioned on Monitoring Using Application Integration Framework in SAP S/4HANA | SAP Help Portal

Reprocessing the Replication

If there are any errors, you can reprocess the replication by selecting the log message and clicking the restart button. It is important to note that you cannot change the payload.

Also, kindly check Monitoring Using FSM Connector | SAP Help Portal 

Hope this helps!

Kind Regards,

Shubham

entry.title: Re: How to create an IM PI document for a SLOC which is t...
entry.content:
Create a Physical Inventory Count:
Go to MI09 (Create Inventory Count without Reference).
Enter the Plant and Storage Location (SLOC).
Add the material number and batch (if applicable).
Enter the count date and count class as needed.
Perform the Inventory Count:
Record the actual stock count in the system.
Post the Inventory Difference:
Use MI10 to post the inventory differences. This will update the stock levels accordingly.
